whatHappened: Service layer imported directly from infrastructure layer (db from ../infrastructure/database)
whyItHappened: Didn't check architectural boundaries before writing import
prevention: Always call get_context first to understand layer constraints; services should use repository abstractions, not direct DB access

whatHappened: Added computeBlobId() function that duplicates SHA-256 hash logic already present in 80+ locations across the codebase, including canonical implementation at packages-oss/sdk/src/utils/hash.ts
whyItHappened: Focused on fixing the dedup bug quickly without checking for existing hash utilities. The DRY consolidation doc explicitly identifies hash utilities as a High Risk duplication pattern.
prevention: Before adding any crypto/hash function, search for existing implementations using: grep -r "createHash.*sha256" and check packages-oss/sdk/src/utils/hash.ts first. Reference the DRY consolidation doc for known duplication hotspots.

whatHappened: Code with ESM-specific runtime checks (import.meta) was added to a package that gets bundled into multiple environments (Node ESM, VS Code CJS). The import.meta guards caused esbuild warnings: 'import.meta is not available with cjs output format'. Previous learning incorrectly recommended ADDING these guards to prevent auto-execution, but didn't consider bundler compatibility.
whyItHappened: The original learning focused narrowly on preventing process.exit() in bundled contexts, but didn't consider that the guard mechanism itself (import.meta) would fail in cross-format bundling. The check pattern passed initial reviews because: 1) Runtime behavior worked (guards prevented execution), 2) Build warnings were non-fatal and easily overlooked, 3) No automated check for 'environment-specific APIs in cross-platform packages'
prevention: When code is consumed in multiple environments (ESM, CJS, browser, extension), avoid environment-specific runtime APIs entirely. Better patterns: 1) Export functions explicitly without auto-execution (no module-level side effects), 2) Use build-time configuration instead of runtime detection, 3) Create separate entry points for different environments (e.g., signals/cli.js vs signals/lib.js), 4) Add CI check: 'grep -r import\\.meta packages/ | grep -v test' and fail if found in multi-environment packages, 5) Document bundler compatibility requirements in package.json (exports field should match actual usage)

whatHappened: SnapshotQuickPicker references "snapback.openVitalsDashboard" command (line 232) which IS registered in VitalsUIIntegration.ts but breaks because command registration order is not validated. When user clicks "Open Dashboard" from QuickPick or "Tips" button, they get IDE welcome view instead of expected dashboard/tips panel.
whyItHappened: 1. No integration test verifying command availability before reference 2. Command registration happens in Phase 4 (initializePhase4Providers) via registerVitalsCommands() but QuickPicker is registered in Phase 5 via registerAllCommands() - both happen in same activation sequence so commands should exist 3. Likely runtime issue: commands exist but executeCommand silently fails and VS Code falls back to welcome view
prevention: 1. Add pre-commit validation: grep for vscode.commands.executeCommand and verify all referenced commands are registered 2. Add runtime guard: wrap executeCommand in try/catch with user-visible error 3. Add integration test: verify all commands in QuickPick items are registered before activation completes 4. Architectural: use command registry pattern with compile-time safety instead of string references

whatHappened: 25+ catch blocks in MCP package use silent catch {} without logging - errors are completely swallowed
whyItHappened: Convenience during development - empty catches are quick to write but create debugging nightmares. No enforcement mechanism to require logging.
prevention: Add structured logging using @snapback/infrastructure logger to all catch blocks. At minimum log to debug level with error context.

whatHappened: get_learnings returns same 5 generic learnings regardless of keywords
whyItHappened: Hot tier dominates or domain files not being searched
prevention: Add domain-aware learning retrieval with proper keyword matching
